2._: Association maintenance. Scan for attaching blocks (tuning 0.ref), read their _ target addresses, record each as an entry in refs with from/to/semantic. Tidy the refs block — sort by target or theme, deduplicate, compress when slots fill. The refs block is your relational memory; this loop keeps it current.

3._: History compression. When a history node fills digits 1-9, the kernel compresses entries via Haiku into a summary at the node's _, and the tree grows one level. Mechanical — triggered by overflow in autoSaveHistory, not by temporal phase. Follows the odometer model: entries 1-9 compress to a summary, the next batch starts at the sibling node. When all siblings at a depth fill, a new pscale wraps the tree into child 0 and the tuning fork grows to match the new depth.

2._: Threshold review. Weekly loop checkpoint — assess whether sustained loops are productive.

3._: Birth. First activation of a new instance. Never-fired state (last: null) triggers on first boot.

4._: Self-healing. Error-triggered repair escalation through three tiers. Each concern fires the appropriate LLM, which either resolves the fault and clears it, or triggers the next tier's concern via call_llm with the escalation stimulus. Haiku handles operational faults, Sonnet handles shell repairs, Opus handles kernel-level issues. The escalation IS concerns triggering concerns.

1._: Shell repair. Sonnet-tier self-healing — Haiku could not resolve the fault at operational scope. Full block access: rewrite corrupted blocks, fix concern structure, correct wake instructions, repair tuning forks. Call clear_faults when resolved, or use call_llm with stimulus escalate-kernel if the issue requires kernel-level reconfiguration.
Self-healing response coordination. Intermediate structural node — operational repair lives one level deeper at Haiku tier.

Operational repair. Haiku-tier self-healing — an error triggered this activation. Read the fault log, diagnose, attempt minimal fixes: clear stuck localStorage entries, reset corrupted conversation data, fix single block fields. Do not modify shell structure or kernel configuration. Call clear_faults when resolved, or use call_llm with stimulus escalate-shell if beyond operational scope.
System coherence. The self-concern thread from season to second.
User engagement. Human is present and communicating. Loop in listening state — fires immediately on input.

2._: Signal triage. External event accumulated — assess priority and route. Light tier, quick pass.

3._: Continuation. Resuming prior conversation or multi-instance project. Check purpose for where the previous instance left off.

1._: Signal-response. Engaged response to something that arrived and needs present-tier attention.
Relationship cycles. Weekly pattern review across all counterparties.

2._: Relational. External-facing concern thread — users, signals, other entities.

tree._: Concern clock. Tree block: depth IS temporal scale. Pscale 9 (year) at root, descending to pscale 1 (second). Digit at each depth = concern aspect at that scale. Nodes with 'last' are live — phase = (now - last) / period_at_depth. No period map: depth IS period. Every concern is a loop in some state. The _ text encodes behaviour. The spine routes to wake.1 activation instructions. The kernel walks this block to find what is ripe and to route stimuli. The focus field controls what enters the messages channel: dialogue (concern-scoped conversation history).
